title: Lotononis peduncularis
intro: Lotononis peduncularis, commonly known as the "Cape sutherlandia," is a perennial shrub native to South Africa, characterized by its woody stems and yellow or white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used to promote health and well-being, with reported health benefits including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to improved cardiovascular health and immune function. The bioactive constituents of Lotononis peduncularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Lotononis peduncularis is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is commonly used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Lotononis peduncularis is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes or pea family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The genus Lotononis is part of the subfamily Faboideae, which includes many other legume species. Lotononis peduncularis is native to Australia and is often found in wet environments. The classification of Lotononis peduncularis reflects its evolutionary relationships with other legumes.
